members.docComment:
/**
 * Explicitly allow plaintext persistence when encrypt/decrypt are identity.
 *
 * In production builds, plaintext persistence is blocked unless this is true.
 */
members.name: allowPlaintext
checksum

members.docComment:
/**
 * Optional decryption hook for persisted payloads.
 *
 * Default is identity (no encryption). Data is stored in plaintext.
 */
members.name: decrypt

members.docComment:
/**
 * Optional async decryption hook for persisted payloads.
 *
 * When provided, persistence will hydrate asynchronously after store creation.
 */
members.name: decryptAsync

members.docComment:
/**
 * Optional encryption hook for persisted payloads.
 *
 * Default is identity (no encryption). Data is stored in plaintext.
 */
members.name: encrypt

members.docComment:
/**
 * Optional async encryption hook for persisted payloads.
 *
 * When provided, persistence will encrypt in the background and hydrate asynchronously.
 */
members.name: encryptAsync
maxSize

members.docComment:
/**
 * Marks this store's persisted data as sensitive (secrets/PII).
 *
 * When `true`, stroid throws at store creation time unless a non-identity `encrypt` hook is provided.
 */
members.name: sensitiveData
store
features

members.docComment:
/**
 * Allow `setStore(name, path, value)` to create missing **leaf** keys on object nodes.
 *
 * Default: `false` (strict path writes).
 *
 * Notes: - Does not expand arrays (out-of-bounds indices are still rejected). - Does not create missing intermediate objects for deep paths; define the shape up-front.
 */
members.name: pathCreate

members.docComment:
/**

 */
members.name: schema

members.docComment:
/**
 * Snapshot cloning strategy used by subscriptions and selector snapshots.
 *
 * - "deep" (default): deep clone and dev-freeze snapshot values. - "shallow": shallow clone (top-level) only; nested references are shared. - "ref": return the live store reference (dev-freeze by default).
 */
members.name: snapshot
snapshotSafety

members.docComment:
/**
 * Optional guard to prevent rapid feedback loops when sync updates trigger local reactions.
 *
 * - true: enable with a default window (100ms) - { windowMs }: customize the guard window in milliseconds - false: disable (default is enabled when sync is truthy)
 */
members.name: loopGuard
